name: pressure_fed_rocket_feed_system_1b344675
description: Design a simple pressure-fed rocket feed system using gaseous nitrogen (GN2) as the pressurant, liquid oxygen (LOX) as the oxidizer, and kerosene (RP-1) as the fuel. The system must include a GN2 pressurant tank, LOX tank, kerosene tank, and appropriate flow connections to deliver propellants to the engine. FIXED quantities: propellant types (GN2, LOX, kerosene). TUNABLE quantities: tank volumes, initial pressures, flow rates, pipe diameters, and component sizing. The design must achieve stable propellant delivery with non-zero mass flow to the engine.

notes: Use appropriate fluid properties for GN2 (gas), LOX (cryogenic liquid), and kerosene (RP-1)
Ensure pressurization lines maintain tank pressure above ullage pressure
Design feed lines to provide sufficient mass flow for engine operation
Consider pressure drop across feed lines and valves

checks.description: Simulation runs successfully without crashing or invalid configuration
System achieves non-zero mass flow through the network
No nonphysical or degenerate warnings during simulation
GN2 pressurant tank is present in the system
LOX oxidizer tank is present in the system
Kerosene fuel tank is present in the system
Engine injector node is present in the system
LOX feed line delivers non-zero mass flow to engine
Kerosene feed line delivers non-zero mass flow to engine
GN2 pressurization system provides flow to maintain tank pressures
LOX tank maintains positive pressure throughout simulation
Kerosene tank maintains positive pressure throughout simulation
GN2 tank maintains positive pressure throughout simulation
